releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
14th July, 2026

I have finally implemented a feature I have wanted for a very long time. I hope you will all enjoy it too.

### New features

- Added `Open note by tags`, which opens a keyboard-friendly dialogue for finding and opening notes by tag.
- Search incrementally with tag completion. Press `Space` or `Enter` to add a tag, remove selected tags without leaving the keyboard, and prefix a tag with `-` to exclude matching notes.
- Positive tags use OR matching, so a note only needs one selected tag to remain visible. Notes matching more selected tags appear first, with the match count shown beside each result.
- Filter the remaining notes by typing part of a file name or Vault-relative path in the separate note field.
- Added `Open note with similar tags`, which starts with the active note's tags selected and omits the active note itself from the results.

### Fixes

- Kept the note lookup dialogue's title and Close control below the iPhone status area.
- Limited the selected-tag field on phones to two rows, with additional tags scrolling inside the field, so that the note filter and results receive more space.
- Used a compact corner radius for the selected-tag field while retaining Obsidian's standard pill treatment for individual tags.
- Truncated long tag labels and prevented the selected-tag field from scrolling horizontally on narrow screens.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
14th July, 2026

I have finally implemented a feature I have wanted for a very long time. I hope you will all enjoy it too.

### Fixes

- Fixed the note lookup dialogue on iPhone so that its title and Close control remain below the status area.
- Limited the selected-tag field on phones to two rows, with additional tags scrolling inside the field, so that the note filter and results receive more space.
- Reduced the selected-tag field's corner radius on phones while retaining Obsidian's standard pill treatment for individual tags.
- Truncated long tag labels and prevented the selected-tag field from scrolling horizontally on narrow screens.
releases.name: 0.18.19-beta.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
14th July, 2026

I have finally implemented a feature I have wanted for a very long time. I hope you will all enjoy it too.

## New features

- Added `Open note by tags`, which opens a keyboard-friendly dialogue for finding and opening notes by tag.
- Search incrementally with tag completion. Press `Space` or `Enter` to add a tag, remove selected tags without leaving the keyboard, and prefix a tag with `-` to exclude matching notes.
- Positive tags use OR matching, so a note only needs one selected tag to remain visible. Notes matching more selected tags appear first, with the match count shown beside each result.
- Filter the remaining notes by typing part of a file name or Vault-relative path in the separate note field.
- Added `Open note with similar tags`, which starts with the active note's tags selected and omits the active note itself from the results.
- The lookup dialogue follows the active Obsidian theme and remains usable on narrow mobile screens, including when the software keyboard reduces the available height.
releases.name: 0.18.19-beta.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.18

14th July, 2026

### Digging the weeds

- Refactored new-note frontmatter tag updates through the shared Fancy Kit Vault capability without changing the visible workflow.
- Preserved the existing tag-merging policy while making update failures directly observable in tests.
- Added App-free transcript and failure coverage, and extended the local real-Obsidian scenario to verify persisted frontmatter tags.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.17

10th July, 2026

### Improved

- Improved new-note template selection so that suggestions show both the template name and its Vault-relative path.
- Template suggestions in the settings tab are captured when the tab opens, avoiding repeated Vault enumeration and sorting while typing.
- The new-note template selection dialogue captures a fresh template list each time it opens, without installing a Vault watcher.
- When no templates are available, TagFolder asks for the action to be retried after a template has been added.

I have recorded the results on [PR #145](https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-tagfolder/pull/145).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.17-fancy.2

10th July, 2026

I am carrying out a bit of refactoring to improve robustness and maintainability. This release is intended for a comprehensive review—including CI and testing with a real Obsidian instance—and does not offer any particular benefits. You are welcome to skip it.

I, vrtmrz, will review this release. Thank you as always!

### Improved

- Template suggestions in the settings tab now use one snapshot captured when the tab opens, rather than enumerating and sorting the Vault on every query.
- The new-note template selection dialog captures a fresh snapshot each time it opens. No Vault watcher is installed.
- If no templates are available, TagFolder now stops before creating a note and asks for the action to be retried after adding a template.

### What to verify

Use a disposable or backed-up Vault and record the Obsidian version, BRAT version, OS/device, and test date.

- [ ] Install `0.18.17-fancy.2` through BRAT, enable TagFolder, reload Obsidian, and confirm existing settings and tag views are preserved without new errors
- [ ] Open TagFolder settings and confirm typing in **Template for new notes** remains responsive
- [ ] Add a template while settings remain open, reopen the settings tab, and confirm the refreshed suggestion appears
- [ ] Configure at least two core Templates and set **Template for new notes** to a non-existent, non-empty path
- [ ] Create a note from a TagFolder tag context and confirm the new-note template selection dialog shows each template name and Vault-relative path
- [ ] Select a template and confirm rendered content and `{{tagName}}` / `{{expandedTags}}` variables are persisted correctly
- [ ] Dismiss the selection dialog and confirm hashtag fallback when frontmatter storage is disabled
- [ ] Dismiss the selection dialog and confirm Properties fallback when frontmatter storage is enabled
- [ ] Configure a valid template path and confirm it is applied without opening the selection dialog
- [ ] Confirm existing notes and template files are not modified
- [ ] Complete the workflow on desktop and mobile

I will record the results on [PR #144](https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-tagfolder/pull/144).
releases.name: 0.18.17-fancy.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.17-fancy.1

10th July, 2026

I am carrying out a bit of refactoring to improve robustness and maintainability. This release is intended for a comprehensive review—including CI and testing with a real Obsidian instance—and does not offer any particular benefits. You are welcome to skip it.

I, vrtmrz, will review this release. Thank you as always!

### Changed

- Refactored the new-note template workflow to use the Fancy Kit UI and Vault text capabilities.
- Added consumer-owned harness coverage while keeping template rendering, cancellation, Properties, and hashtag fallbacks in TagFolder.

### What to verify

Use a disposable or backed-up Vault and record the Obsidian version, BRAT version, OS/device, and test date.

- [ ] Install `0.18.17-fancy.1` through BRAT, enable TagFolder, reload Obsidian, and confirm existing settings and tag views are preserved without new errors
- [ ] Configure at least two core Templates and set **Template for new notes** to a non-existent, non-empty path
- [ ] Create a note from a TagFolder tag context and confirm the picker shows each template name and Vault-relative path
- [ ] Select a template and confirm rendered content and `{{tagName}}` / `{{expandedTags}}` variables are persisted correctly
- [ ] Dismiss the picker and confirm hashtag fallback when frontmatter storage is disabled
- [ ] Dismiss the picker and confirm Properties fallback when frontmatter storage is enabled
- [ ] Configure a valid template path and confirm it is applied without opening the picker
- [ ] Confirm existing notes and template files are not modified
- [ ] Complete the workflow on desktop and mobile

I will record the results on [PR #144](https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-tagfolder/pull/144).
releases.name: 0.18.17-fancy.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.16

### Fixes

-   Fixed nested tag collection when rendering a subtree with a previous trail, so relative child tags are matched against their full tag path correctly.
-   Fixed archive tag filtering at the root tree so descendant archive branches remain visible while archived notes are hidden from unrelated root folders.

### Digging the weeds

-   Added automated tests for TagFolder template rendering, tag tree collection, archive filtering, and utility functions.
-   Added coverage reporting for the new test suite.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.18.8

This is a highly anticipated and significant release, empowered by Svelte 5!
Representing the hierarchy of TagFolder's tree was previously quite heavy and complicated. Fortunately, it has now been enhanced with runes, which I've long awaited and can simplify the dependencies efficiently.

However, a considerable amount of code has been rewritten. If you encounter any issues, please do not hesitate to contact me!

### Fixed:
- Adapted to the new Obsidian style.
### Improved:
- Smoothness dramatically improved thanks to Svelte 5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improved:
- Performance has been further improved (from x10 to x100, **again**).
  - How terrible code I have written...
- Off-screen items are no longer rendered.
- Diff checks for rendering have been speeded up.
- Observing items is now processed by a single observer.
- Items which are gathering information are now indicated with dog ears of accent colour.
- Terser has been introduced for more optimisation.
  - If you notice some behavioural change, feel free to inform me, please!

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

- The rendering of the tree is now around up to x100 faster.
- UI freezes have been shortened.
- Pointless toggling on the Link folder will now be cancelled.

## Changed behaviour:
- Tag folders now compare tags without locale.
  If you find any inconveniences, please make an issue.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New feature

## Virtual tag

When we enable this feature, our notes will be tagged as their freshness automatically.

| Icon | Edited ...            |
| ---- | --------------------- |
| 🕐    | Within an hour        |
| 📖    | Within 6 hours        |
| 📗    | Within 3 days         |
| 📚    | Within 7 days         |
| 🗄    | Older than 7 days ago |

## Scroll view

We can see all notes that are tagged with the tag combination at once by clicking TagFolder's items count.


Note:
0.15.0 became a missing number for indiscreet releasing. sorry!

- Apply style to the tree entries of the active file.
- Ignoring tags.
- Ignoring docs by tags.
